Probably my preferred option. Its far more rigid than the plastic gantry and allows identical print quality to the MK3.1. Downside being its a bit more complicated and about 20$ more expensive. You also have to source the aluminum parts.
Can be Misumi extrusion or MakerBeam.
DXF is provided on github. Should be made from 3mm(1/8in) 5052 aluminum. Has a single 90 degree bend. Can be obtained from sendcutsend or I have a small stock I can provide at cost on discord.

DXF is provided on github. Should be made from 3mm(1/8in) 5052 aluminum. Get this tumbled unless you want to deburr it yourself. Can be obtained from sendcutsend or I have a small stock I can provide at cost on the discord.

DXF is provided on github. Should be made from 3mm(1/8in) 5052 aluminum. Get this deburred unless you want to deburr it yourself. Can be obtained from sendcutsend or I have a small stock I can provide at cost on the discord.

Easier to source for, cheaper, and simplier since it uses only 3D printed parts in place of aluminum. The downside being it is unsurprisingly way less rigid and will result in more deflection and worse print quality at speed.
